report_name: Ransomware Attack on Illinois High School Disables Safety Systems, Forcing Campus Shutdown

threat_actors.name: Unnamed Ransomware Actor
threat_actors.description: The unidentified threat actor responsible for the ransomware attack against Evanston Township High School on June 7, 2026, which crippled both IT and physical safety systems.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The attackers deployed ransomware to encrypt critical servers, including those managing the school's IT and physical security systems, to cause maximum disruption.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The attackers likely deleted volume shadow copies using tools like vssadmin.exe to prevent easy restoration of encrypted files from local backups.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit Response Function
attack_patterns.description: The ransomware attack disabled critical building safety systems, including door access controls and public address (PA) systems, preventing the school from managing physical security or communicating in an emergency.

tools.name: vssadmin
tools.description: The tool vssadmin.exe was likely used by the attackers to delete volume shadow copies to inhibit system recovery, a common tactic in ransomware attacks.

identities.name: Evanston Township High School
identities.description: A large high school in Illinois that was the victim of a ransomware attack, forcing it to shut down its campus due to the compromise of physical safety systems.

identities.name: FBI
identities.description: The Federal Bureau of Investigation was engaged by the school district to investigate the ransomware attack.

identities.name: PowerSchool
identities.description: Provider of the Home Access Center student portal for ETHS, which was taken offline as a result of the ransomware attack.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 9c5c8f8d-1c3a-4b1e-8f3b-2d7e5a9c1b2a
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware TTP to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1490/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-10
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative activity, although rare.
level: high
indicators.description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware technique to inhibit system recovery mentioned as a hunting hint in the article.
